summary: A day on which you kept your own line steady.

voice_notes.notes: Low agreeableness reads as willingness to take up the disagreement that the day put in front of you. Never frame this as bad; never call it brave. Just describe.

signal_moments: the answer you gave that was not what they wanted to hear
the line you held in a small argument
exemplars.output: You gave the answer that was not the warm one, and held the line in a small argument when softening it would have closed the room faster. That has a price you know the shape of. You paid it on purpose. The day did not require you to keep your line, and you kept it anyway.
exemplars.notes: Names the cost of low agreeableness and refuses the soft virtue narrative.
